The private sector's contribution also reached an all-time high, bolstering defense exports.

title: Shreyas Iyer surpasses Virat Kohli, becomes joint second-fastest Indian to...

This places him alongside Shikhar Dhawan and ahead of Virat Kohli. The achievement occurred during India's second ODI against Afghanistan in Lucknow. Shubman Gill remains the fastest Indian to this mark.

India coach Sairaj Bahutule backed Sooryavanshi, saying the teenager would learn from the episode.

Fuel shortages and soaring inflation have receded, with economic growth returning and tourism booming. While international lenders praise the IMF-backed reforms, significant challenges like high public debt and low investment persist, indicating recovery is still incomplete and transformation is the next crucial step.

title: US redraws AI rules, treating software like missile technology

It suggests AI is now considered strategic infrastructure, not just software.

description: India's defence production has surged to a record Rs 1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26, a significant leap towards self-reliance. Prime Minister Modi highlighted this achievement, showcasing indigenous platforms like Tejas, Akash, Agni-V, and INS Vikrant. This marks a major transformation from import dependence to a capable defence exporter.

Minor girl forced into illegal conversion and marriage; Kutch police arrest Ajmer Dargah Imam
description: Rapar police have arrested an Imam from Ajmer for allegedly facilitating the illegal religious conversion and Nikah of a 17-year-old girl. The girl's family reported her abduction by Jahin Abdul Chauhan, who allegedly assaulted her and forced her into marriage after conversion. Both accused are in police custody.

'I killed her': Bengaluru man calls lawyer after strangling wife
description: Bengaluru police have arrested a water tanker driver, Sharath, accused of strangling his 20-year-old wife, Anusha, following frequent domestic quarrels. The accused allegedly confessed to the crime over a phone call to an advocate before fleeing their Malleswaram residence. Police discovered the victim's body after being alerted by the advocate and subsequently launched a manhunt for Sharath.

Meet Antonela Roccuzzo, Lionel Messi’s childhood love
description: Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo share one of sport's most enduring love stories, rooted in their shared childhood in Rosario, Argentina. They married in July 2017 and have three sons together: Thiago, Mateo, and Ciro. Roccuzzo has built a massive global following in her own right, known less for glamour and more for consistent family presence through every chapter of Messi's extraordinary career. As Messi competes at the 2026 World Cup, their family remains one of the most recognised in football.

CJP founder assault case: Out on bail, accused welcomed with garlands
description: Five men accused of assaulting Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Deepke during a Jaipur protest were granted bail Tuesday. The accused, detained for breach of peace, were released on Rs 20,000 personal bonds with six-month restrictions. An argument during the demonstration reportedly escalated into a physical altercation.

title: Flight of the 'seagull': Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman in space 63 years ago today
description: On June 16, 1963, Valentina Tereshkova, a 26-year-old textile worker, became the first woman in space aboard Vostok 6. Her flight, a political statement by Nikita Khrushchev, lasted nearly three days, completing 48 orbits. Tereshkova's achievement, despite lacking formal pilot or engineering training, was celebrated globally, though she never flew again.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
description: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.
